title: ceradenia mayoris
intro: Ceradenia mayoris, also known as the Mayor's Ceradenia, is a species of epiphytic fern native to Central and South America, specifically found in the tropical regions of Costa Rica and Panama. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Ceradenia mayoris include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate coughs, and promote overall lung health. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are steeped in hot water or mixed with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
